title: Immortality
content:
I bowed my head in anguish sore
    When Life made Death his bride;
    �Soul, we are lost forever more!�
    Unto my soul I cried.

    �Nay, waste in wailing not thy breath,�
    My soul replied to me,
    �Behold! The child of Life and Death
    Is Immortality!�
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ellis Parker Butler, titled "Immortality", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
